## Quick Start Guide

# https://github.com/roboflow-ai/bob

# Quick-start guide for AutoDocs

## Minimal Setup Steps

1. Install required dependencies
```bash
pip install watsonx-client
pip install pathlib
pip install typing
```

2. Import the AutoDocs class
```python
from autodocs import AutoDocs
```

3. Create a WatsonxClient instance
```python
from watsonx_client import WatsonxClient

watsonx_client = WatsonxClient(
    api_key="your_watsonx_api_key",
    project_id="your_project_id"
)
```

4. Initialize AutoDocs
```python
autodocs = AutoDocs(watsonx_client)
```

## Basic Usage Example

Generate a quick-start guide for a Python file:

```python
file_path = "path/to/your/python/file.py"
doc_type = "quick_start"

documentation = asyncio.run(autodocs.generate_docs(file_path, doc_type))
print(documentation)
```

## Next Steps

1. Generate comprehensive documentation for your codebase
```python
documentation = asyncio.run(autodocs.generate_docs("path/to/file.py", "full"))
print(documentation)
```

2. Explore different documentation types available in AutoDocs
- User Manual
- How-To Guide
- Tutorial
- Troubleshooting Guide
- User Persona
- Knowledge Base Article
- UX Design Documentation
- Wireframe Documentation
- Software Requirements Specification
- API Documentation

3. Customize the documentation generation process
- Modify the prompts in `lib/prompts.py`
- Adjust the formatting in `_format_documentation` method
- Implement additional generator functions in `lib/generators.py`

## Links to Detailed Documentation

- AutoDocs Class: [AutoDocs.py](https://github.com/roboflow-ai/bob/blob/main/autodocs/AutoDocs.py)
- WatsonxClient: [watsonx-client documentation](https://github.com/roboflow-ai/watsonx-client-python)
- Pathlib Module: [Python pathlib documentation](https://docs.python.org/3/library/pathlib.html)
- Typing Module: [Python typing documentation](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html)
- asyncio Module: [Python asyncio documentation](https://docs.python.org/3/library/asyncio.html)

## User Manual

# Version: 0.1.0

```python
# Auto Docs - Core Documentation Generator

## Overview and Purpose
Auto Docs is an automated documentation generation system for code. It uses AI (Watsonx) to create comprehensive documentation for code files. The system supports 12 different documentation types for various use cases, including user manuals, how-to guides, tutorials, troubleshooting guides, and more. It features optimizations like concurrent generation, file hash-based caching, and extracted prompts for easier maintenance.

## Installation Instructions
1. Ensure you have Python installed on your system.
2. Clone the repository or download the code files.
3. Install the required dependencies using pip:
   ```
   pip install -r requirements.txt
   ```
4. Set up the Watsonx client with your credentials.

## Configuration Options
- `watsonx_client`: An instance of the WatsonxClient for AI-powered documentation generation.
- `enable_cache`: A boolean flag to enable or disable file hash-based caching for performance optimization.

## Usage Examples
### Generating User Manual
```python
from auto_docs import AutoDocs

# Initialize AutoDocs with Watsonx client
auto_docs = AutoDocs(watsonx_client, enable_cache=True)

# Generate user manual for a code file
documentation = auto_docs.generate_docs("path/to/code_file.py", "user_manual")
print(documentation)
```

### Generating API Documentation
```python
from auto_docs import AutoDocs

# Initialize AutoDocs with Watsonx client
auto_docs = AutoDocs(watsonx_client, enable_cache=True)

# Generate API documentation for a code file
documentation = auto_docs.generate_docs("path/to/code_file.py", "api")
print(documentation)
```

## Common Use Cases
- Generating comprehensive user manuals for code projects.
- Creating step-by-step how-to guides for specific functionalities.
- Developing quick-start guides for new users.
- Building interactive tutorials for code learning.
- Creating troubleshooting guides for common issues.
- Generating user personas for better understanding of target users.
- Creating internal knowledge base articles for code documentation.
- Developing UX design documentation for user interfaces.
- Generating wireframes and UI mockups for design documentation.
- Creating software requirement specifications for project planning.
- Generating API documentation for code libraries and frameworks.
- Creating comprehensive documentation covering all types for a complete overview.

## Best Practices
- Use the `enable_cache` option to improve performance by avoiding redundant documentation generation for unchanged files.
- Choose the appropriate documentation type based on your specific needs and use case.
- Ensure that the code files are well-structured and properly commented for better documentation generation.
- Regularly update the documentation to keep it in sync with the code changes.
- Review and refine the generated documentation to ensure accuracy and clarity.
- Use the comprehensive "full" documentation type when you need a complete overview of the code project.

Note: The code provided assumes the existence of certain modules and classes (e.g., `WatsonxClient`, `lib.utils`, `lib.utils.cache`) which are not included in the given code snippet. Make sure to have the necessary dependencies and modules in place for the code to run successfully.

## How-To Guide

# https://github.com/ashaltafi/bob
# https://github.com/ashaltafi/bob/blob/main/autodocs/__init__.py

Objective:
The objective of this AutoDocs code is to generate comprehensive documentation for code using AI-powered documentation generation. It supports 12 different documentation types for various use cases and includes optimizations such as concurrent generation, file hash-based caching, and extracted prompts for easier maintenance.

Prerequisites:
1. Python 3.x installed
2. Required libraries installed (asyncio, typing, pathlib, lib.utils, lib.utils.cache)
3. WatsonxClient instance for AI-powered documentation generation

Step-by-Step Instructions:
1. Import the necessary libraries:
   - asyncio
   - typing (Dict, Any, Optional)
   - pathlib (Path)
   - lib.utils (detect_language, get_timestamp, read_file_safe, format_markdown_header)
   - lib.utils.cache (get_cache)
   - .prompts (get_documentation_prompt)
   - .generators (various generator functions)

2. Define the AutoDocs class:
   - Initialize the class with a WatsonxClient instance and an optional enable_cache parameter (default: True)
   - Set up the cache if enabled using get_cache()
   - Create a dictionary (doc_generators) mapping documentation types to their respective generator functions

3. Implement the generate_docs method:
   - Read the code file using read_file_safe()
   - Check for errors and handle them appropriately
   - Check the cache first if caching is enabled and a cache key exists
   - Detect the language of the code file using detect_language()
   - Generate documentation based on the specified doc_type:
     - If doc_type is "full", generate comprehensive documentation with all types concurrently using _generate_full_documentation()
     - If doc_type is in doc_generators, generate specific documentation type using the corresponding generator function
     - If doc_type is not recognized, fallback to basic documentation generation using "api" prompt
   - Format the generated documentation with metadata using _format_documentation()
   - Cache the result if caching is enabled and a cache key exists

4. Implement the _generate_full_documentation method:
   - Define the documentation types to generate and their corresponding names
   - Create an async function generate_section to generate each section concurrently
   - Use asyncio.gather to execute the section generation concurrently
   - Join the generated sections with newline characters

5. Implement the _format_documentation method:
   - Create a dictionary mapping doc_type to its corresponding name
   - Get the doc_name based on the provided doc_type
   - Format the documentation header using format_markdown_header with file path, language, documentation type, and generated timestamp
   - Return the formatted header concatenated with the generated documentation

Expected Outcomes:
- The AutoDocs class will be initialized with a WatsonxClient instance and optional caching enabled
- The generate_docs method will generate documentation for a specified code file and doc_type
- Comprehensive documentation will be generated for the "full" doc_type, including API documentation, quick start guide, user manual, how-to guide, tutorial, troubleshooting guide, and requirements specification
- Specific documentation types will be generated based on the provided doc_type using the corresponding generator functions
- The generated documentation will be formatted with metadata, including file path, language, documentation type, and generated timestamp
- The generated documentation will be cached if caching is enabled and a cache key exists

Tips and Warnings:
- Ensure that the required libraries are installed before running the code
- Make sure to provide a valid WatsonxClient instance for AI-powered documentation generation
- Be cautious when enabling caching, as it may consume additional storage space
- The code assumes the existence of certain functions and classes from the lib.utils and lib.utils.cache modules, so make sure they are properly implemented and accessible
- The code relies on the availability and reliability of the WatsonxClient's generate_text method for AI-powered documentation generation
- The code uses asyncio for concurrent generation, which may require proper handling of asynchronous operations and error handling
- The code uses markdown formatting for the generated documentation, so ensure that the output is rendered correctly in a markdown-compatible environment

## Tutorial

# https://github.com/PyBob/AutoDocs

Learning Objectives:
1. Understand the purpose and functionality of the AutoDocs class
2. Learn how to initialize an AutoDocs instance
3. Explore the supported documentation types
4. Learn how to generate documentation using the generate_docs method
5. Understand the caching mechanism and its benefits
6. Learn about the concurrent generation of comprehensive documentation
7. Understand the formatting of generated documentation

Prerequisites:
- Basic understanding of Python programming
- Familiarity with asynchronous programming (async/await)
- Knowledge of file handling in Python
- Understanding of caching concepts

Step-by-Step Lessons:

Lesson 1: Introduction to AutoDocs
- AutoDocs is a class that automates the generation of comprehensive documentation for code
- It supports 12 different documentation types for various use cases
- The class uses AI-powered documentation generation through the watsonx_client
- Caching is enabled by default to improve performance

Lesson 2: Initializing an AutoDocs Instance
- To create an instance of AutoDocs, you need to provide a watsonx_client and an optional enable_cache parameter
- Example:
  ```python
  watsonx_client = WatsonxClient()  # Assume this is already initialized
  auto_docs = AutoDocs(watsonx_client, enable_cache=True)
  ```

Lesson 3: Supported Documentation Types
- AutoDocs supports 12 documentation types:
  1. user_manual
  2. how_to_guide
  3. quick_start
  4. tutorial
  5. troubleshooting
  6. user_persona
  7. knowledge_base
  8. ux_design
  9. wireframe
  10. requirements
  11. api
  12. full
- Each type serves a specific purpose and generates documentation accordingly

Lesson 4: Generating Documentation
- Use the generate_docs method to generate documentation for a code file
- Provide the file_path and the desired doc_type
- Example:
  ```python
  documentation = await auto_docs.generate_docs("path/to/code.py", doc_type="tutorial")
  ```

Lesson 5: Caching Mechanism
- AutoDocs supports file hash-based caching to improve performance
- When enable_cache is set to True, the generated documentation is cached based on the file's hash
- If the file remains unchanged, the cached result is returned instead of regenerating the documentation
- Caching can significantly reduce generation time, especially for large projects

Lesson 6: Concurrent Generation of Comprehensive Documentation
- When doc_type is set to "full", AutoDocs generates comprehensive documentation by combining multiple documentation types
- It uses asyncio.gather() to generate sections concurrently, resulting in faster generation times
- The generated sections are then combined into a single comprehensive documentation string

Lesson 7: Formatting of Generated Documentation
- The generated documentation is formatted with metadata using the _format_documentation method
- The metadata includes the documentation name, file path, language, documentation type, and generation timestamp
- The formatted documentation is returned as a string

Practice Exercises:

Exercise 1: Generating User Manual
- Create an instance of AutoDocs
- Generate a user manual for a sample code file
- Print the generated documentation

Exercise 2: Generating Multiple Documentation Types
- Create an instance of AutoDocs
- Generate documentation for different types (e.g., tutorial, quick_start, troubleshooting) for a sample code file
- Print the generated documentation for each type

Exercise 3: Exploring Caching
- Create an instance of AutoDocs with caching enabled
- Generate documentation for a code file
- Modify the code file slightly
- Generate documentation again and observe if caching is utilized

Summary:
- AutoDocs is a powerful class that automates the generation of comprehensive documentation for code
- It supports 12 different documentation types and uses AI-powered generation through watsonx_client
- Caching is enabled by default to improve performance and reduce generation time
- Comprehensive documentation can be generated concurrently using asyncio.gather()
- The generated documentation is formatted with relevant metadata for easy reference

Next Steps:
- Explore the individual documentation types and their specific use cases
- Experiment with generating documentation for different code files and observe the results
- Investigate ways to customize the prompts used for AI-powered documentation generation
- Consider integrating AutoDocs into your development workflow to streamline documentation processes
- Explore advanced features and optimizations mentioned in the code comments, such as file hash-based caching and prompt extraction

## Troubleshooting Guide

# https://www.bobpowered.com


# Common Errors and Solutions

1. File not found error
   - Ensure the file path is correct and the file exists
   - Check if the user has permission to read the file

2. Unsupported file type
   - Ensure the file is a supported programming language
   - Add support for the language by creating a new generator in the generators module

3. Watsonx AI service unavailable
   - Check your internet connection
   - Ensure you have a valid Watsonx API key
   - Contact IBM support if the issue persists

4. Caching not working
   - Ensure caching is enabled during initialization
   - Check if the cache directory has the correct permissions
   - Verify the cache key generation logic

5. Generation timeout
   - Increase the timeout value in the WatsonxClient configuration
   - Simplify the code being documented if possible
   - Contact IBM support for further assistance

# Debugging Steps

1. Verify the input file
   - Check if the file exists and is readable
   - Ensure the file contains valid code

2. Check the language detection
   - Print the detected language
   - Verify if the language is supported

3. Inspect the generated prompts
   - Print the prompts sent to Watsonx AI
   - Verify if the prompts are correctly formatted

4. Monitor the AI generation process
   - Print the raw AI-generated content
   - Check if the content is relevant and complete

5. Validate the formatted output
   - Inspect the final formatted documentation
   - Ensure all required sections are present

6. Check the caching mechanism
   - Verify if cached results are being returned
   - Inspect the cache key generation logic

7. Profile the performance
   - Use profiling tools to identify bottlenecks
   - Check if concurrent generation is working as expected

# FAQ

Q: What programming languages are supported?
A: AutoDocs supports all languages that can be detected by the detect_language function. Currently, it supports common languages like Python, JavaScript, Java, C++, etc.

Q: Can I customize the documentation prompts?
A: Yes, you can customize the prompts by modifying the get_documentation_prompt function in the prompts module. You can also create custom prompts for specific use cases.

Q: How can I improve the documentation quality?
A: To improve documentation quality:
   - Provide clear and concise code comments
   - Use meaningful variable and function names
   - Break down complex code into smaller, well-documented functions
   - Experiment with different temperature values for AI generation

Q: Is there a limit to the size of the code file?
A: While there's no hard limit, very large files may cause performance issues or time out. For optimal results, keep your code files under 10,000 lines.

Q: Can I generate documentation for multiple files at once?
A: Currently, AutoDocs processes one file at a time. You can create a script to iterate through multiple files and call the generate_docs method for each file.

# Known Issues

1. Inconsistent documentation quality
   - The quality of AI-generated documentation may vary based on the complexity of the code and the clarity of comments
   - Manual review and editing of generated documentation is recommended

2. Limited support for obscure programming languages
   - AutoDocs may not support very old or niche programming languages
   - Adding support for new languages requires creating new generators in the generators module

3. Potential for inaccurate or irrelevant content
   - In rare cases, the AI may generate content that is not relevant to the code
   - This can be mitigated by providing clear prompts and reviewing the generated content

4. Caching inconsistencies
   - In some cases, the caching mechanism may not work as expected
   - This can be due to issues with the cache directory permissions or cache key generation

5. Performance issues with very large codebases
   - Generating comprehensive documentation for large codebases may be slow
   - Consider generating documentation for specific modules or files instead of the entire codebase

# Support Resources

1. Official Documentation
   - Visit the AutoDocs documentation website for detailed guides and examples
   - URL: https://autodocs.example.com

2. GitHub Repository
   - Explore the AutoDocs source code and contribute to its development
   - URL: https://github.com/example/autodocs

3. IBM Watsonx Support
   - Contact IBM support for issues related to the Watsonx AI service
   - URL: https://www.ibm.com/watsonx/support

4. Community Forum
   - Join the AutoDocs community forum to ask questions and share experiences
   - URL: https://forum.example.com/autodocs

5. Email Support
   - For critical issues or enterprise support, contact AutoDocs support via email
   - Email: support@autodocs.example.com

6. Troubleshooting Guide
   - Refer to the built-in troubleshooting guide for common issues and solutions
   - Access it by running: AutoDocs.generate_docs("troubleshooting_guide")

Remember to always keep your AutoDocs library up to date for the latest features and bug fixes. If you encounter any issues not covered in this guide, don't hesitate to reach out to the support resources provided.

## Requirements Specification

# https://github.com/Code-Bob-AI/Code-Bob

The AutoDocs system is designed to automatically generate comprehensive documentation for codebases using AI. Below is a detailed Software Requirements Specification (SRS) for the AutoDocs system based on the provided code.

## 1. Introduction

### 1.1 Purpose
The purpose of this SRS is to provide a detailed description of the AutoDocs system, which generates comprehensive documentation for code using AI. This document outlines the functional and non-functional requirements, system constraints, dependencies, and acceptance criteria for the system.

### 1.2 Scope
The AutoDocs system will generate documentation for various types, including user manuals, how-to guides, quick start guides, tutorials, troubleshooting guides, user personas, knowledge base articles, UX design documentation, wireframes, software requirements specifications, and API documentation. The system will support concurrent generation for performance optimization and file hash-based caching for efficiency.

### 1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, and Abbreviations
- **AI**: Artificial Intelligence
- **SRS**: Software Requirements Specification
- **API**: Application Programming Interface
- **SRS**: Software Requirements Specification

## 2. Overall Description

### 2.1 Product Perspective
AutoDocs is a standalone system that integrates with the Watsonx AI platform to generate documentation for code. It can be integrated into existing development workflows to automate the documentation process.

### 2.2 Product Functions
- Generate comprehensive documentation for code using AI.
- Support multiple documentation types.
- Concurrent generation for performance optimization.
- File hash-based caching for efficiency.
- Format the generated documentation with metadata.

### 2.3 User Characteristics
- Developers who need to generate documentation for their code.
- Technical writers who assist in creating documentation.
- Project managers who require comprehensive documentation for project deliverables.

### 2.4 Operating Environment
- The system can be run on any platform that supports Python.
- Requires access to the Watsonx AI platform for documentation generation.

### 2.5 Design and Implementation Constraints
- The system relies on the Watsonx AI platform for documentation generation.
- The system is implemented in Python.

### 2.6 User Documentation
- User manuals and guides will be generated by the system itself.

### 2.7 Assumptions and Dependencies
- The system assumes access to the Watsonx AI platform.
- The system depends on the Python programming language for implementation.

## 3. External Interface Requirements

### 3.1 User Interfaces
- Command-line interface for interacting with the AutoDocs system.
- Integration points with the Watsonx AI platform for documentation generation.

### 3.2 Hardware Interfaces
- No specific hardware interfaces are required.

### 3.3 Software Interfaces
- Integration with the Watsonx AI platform for AI-powered documentation generation.
- Dependency on Python libraries for file handling, caching, and concurrency.

### 3.4 Communications Interfaces
- No specific communication interfaces are required.

## 4. System Features

### 4.1 Functional Requirements

#### F.1 Generate Documentation
- **F.1.1** The system shall generate documentation for a code file.
- **F.1.2** The system shall support multiple documentation types.
- **F.1.3** The system shall generate comprehensive documentation with all types concurrently.
- **F.1.4** The system shall format the generated documentation with metadata.

#### F.2 Caching
- **F.2.1** The system shall support file hash-based caching for performance optimization.

#### F.3 Language Detection
- **F.3.1** The system shall detect the programming language of the code file.

#### F.4 Error Handling
- **F.4.1** The system shall handle errors gracefully and provide meaningful error messages.

### 4.2 Non-Functional Requirements

#### NFR.1 Performance
- **NFR.1.1** The system shall generate documentation concurrently for improved performance.

#### NFR.2 Usability
- **NFR.2.1** The system shall provide a command-line interface for ease of use.

#### NFR.3 Reliability
- **NFR.3.1** The system shall handle errors and provide meaningful error messages.

#### NFR.4 Maintainability
- **NFR.4.1** The system shall be implemented in Python for ease of maintenance.

## 5. System Constraints

### 5.1 System Constraints
- The system relies on the Watsonx AI platform for documentation generation.
- The system is implemented in Python.

## 6. Dependencies

### 6.1 Dependencies
- **Watsonx AI Platform**: The system depends on the Watsonx AI platform for AI-powered documentation generation.
- **Python Libraries**: The system depends on various Python libraries for file handling, caching, and concurrency.

## 7. Acceptance Criteria

### 7.1 Acceptance Criteria
- **AC.1** The system shall generate documentation for a code file.
- **AC.2** The system shall support multiple documentation types.
- **AC.3** The system shall generate comprehensive documentation with all types concurrently.
- **AC.4** The system shall format the generated documentation with metadata.
- **AC.5** The system shall support file hash-based caching for performance optimization.
- **AC.6** The system shall detect the programming language of the code file.
- **AC.7** The system shall handle errors gracefully and provide meaningful error messages.

## 8. Other Requirements

### 8.1 Other Requirements
- The system shall be easy to integrate into existing development workflows.
- The system shall be scalable to handle large codebases.

This SRS provides a comprehensive overview of the AutoDocs system, detailing its requirements, constraints, dependencies, and acceptance criteria. It serves as a guide for the development team to implement the system effectively.
